text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. This means you clean up the code to make it more organized and easier to understand, but the program still works exactly the same way. Refactoring helps reduce technical debt, which is the extra effort needed later when code becomes messy or confusing. By making small, careful changes—like renaming variables or simplifying complex functions—you increase readability. This makes it easier for you or other developers to modify the code in the future. While refactoring doesn't add new features, it keeps your code base healthy and maintainable over time.
